summary: Large incremental item decomp/matching pass across 30 files, mostly under src/melee/it. The PR moved itsscope.c and it_2E5A.c from NonMatching to Matching, improved many item functions and data sections, and updated symbols/splits to reflect local data labels and tighter section boundaries. The author explicitly framed the code as temporary/gross matching work while closing in on all item TUs. decomp.dev reported +0.40% matched code, +15688 matched-code bytes, 31 new matches, and 59 improvements in unmatched items, with one broken .sdata2 match in itmewtwoshadowball and a few small regressions.
what_changed: Marked melee/it/items/itsscope.c and melee/it/items/it_2E5A.c as Matching in configure.py.
Advanced many item translation units, especially it_2725.c, itsamusgrapple.c, itlinkhookshot.c, itdosei.c, it_2E5A.c, itbombhei.c, and it_266F.c.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to convert several previously global-looking grHeal and item constants/strings to local labels such as @155, @159, @249, and ...data.0.
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt for itmewtwoshadowball.c .sdata end address from 0x804D5580 to 0x804D557D.
Retyped it_802E7654 from taking an s32 owner to taking an Item_GObj* owner, then removed casts at stage callsites in grcorneria.c and grvenom.c.
Added or reshaped local inline helpers/macros for HSD_JObj rotation setters in item code, preserving assert strings/line behavior needed for matching.
Introduced stack and codegen nudges such as PAD_STACK changes, #pragma dont_inline, volatile sqrt storage helpers, and local typedefs for table layout matching.

decomp.dev recorded 31 new matches, including itsscope it_80291DAC, it_2E5A functions/data, several it_2725 functions/.sdata, itbombhei animations, itlinkbomb animations/data, and itsamusgrapple pieces.
smart_moves: Converted object-file status only after enough evidence: itsscope.c and it_2E5A.c were flipped to Matching in configure.py as part of the same diff that supplied symbol/sdata/sdata2 cleanup.
Matched codegen by localizing SDK-like HSD_JObj setter bodies with explicit __assert file/line/message behavior instead of relying only on shared HSD_JObjSetRotationX/Y/Z calls.
Used tiny helper functions/macros to make repeated item animation patterns compile the same way, such as bombhei dynamic-bone translation/rotation helpers and dosei rotation-base helpers.
Preserved pointer types instead of using integer casts where possible: it_802E7654 now accepts Item_GObj*, improving callsites in grcorneria.c and grvenom.c while still serving matching.
Reclassified anonymous constants and strings as local labels in symbols.txt, which is important when matching data boundaries and avoiding fake global names.
Accepted an incremental, slightly ugly matching pass rather than hiding it locally; this made measurable progress visible and reduced the remaining item workload.
decomp_lessons: For item TUs near completion, matching often depends on local data-label scope and exact split boundaries, not just C control flow.
When SDK inline/assert behavior affects codegen, cloning the setter inline locally with the correct assert base string and offsets can be a practical matching tactic.
Retyping function prototypes from integer placeholders to real pointer types can simplify downstream stage/item callsites without necessarily hurting matching.
Stack shape matters: PAD_STACK size changes and volatile temporary storage around sqrtf-style helpers were used as targeted codegen controls.
Short-lived local structs or typedefs can express overlayed global table layout well enough to guide matching, as seen with the ItemSpawnTables wrapper over it_804A0E30.
Progress reports should be checked for regressions: this PR gained many matches but decomp.dev still reported a broken itmewtwoshadowball .sdata2 match and three small unmatched-item regressions.
naming_conventions: Unidentified item functions retained address-style names such as it_8026CD50, it_8026EECC, it_80274740, it_8027C0F0, and it_802E7654.
Anonymous/local data in symbols.txt used @NNN-style names and ...data.0 rather than fake global grHeal_/it_ names when evidence indicated local scope.

Item-specific helper names were prefixed by the surrounding symbol/function or item, for example it_80274740_JObjSetRotationX, it_8027C0F0_JObjSetRotationZ, itDosei_JObjSetRotationX, and it_8026EECC_VARS.
Item motion callbacks kept project-style names such as itBombhei_UnkMotion8_Anim, itLinkbomb_UnkMotion0_Anim, and itDosei_UnkMotion2_Coll.
Typed item object APIs should use Item_GObj* in headers/calls when known, replacing placeholder s32 owner parameters.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inline HSD_JObj rotation setters manually to reproduce assert line numbers and dirty-matrix behavior.
Use char* assert_base variables and pointer offsets into assert string data to match generated assertion operands.
Use volatile float storage helpers around reciprocal-square-root/sqrtf-accurate sequences when stack stores influence matching.
Apply #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around functions that should not be inlined by the compiler.
Tune stack layout explicitly with PAD_STACK, including a documented change from PAD_STACK(4) to PAD_STACK(16) in it_266F.c.
Introduce macro accessors for awkward item variable union layouts, e.g. it_8026EECC_VARS(ip), to force desired pointer access patterns.
Change symbol scope from global to local for anonymous constants/strings in symbols.txt to align data matching.
Remove unnecessary casts at callsites after prototype correction, e.g. passing gobj directly to it_802E7654.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The author warned that some code was getting gross as they approached matching all item TUs and chose to merge incremental item/ work anyway because they were the only active it/ worker.

decomp.dev reported strong net progress: matched code 67.65% (+0.40%, +15688 bytes), linked code 39.07% (+0.16%, +6056 bytes), matched data 39.45% (+0.03%, +400 bytes), and linked data 33.21% (+0.02%, +272 bytes).
decomp.dev also flagged one broken match: main/melee/it/items/itmewtwoshadowball .sdata2 fell from 100.00% to 85.94%, plus three smaller unmatched-item regressions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fix the itmewtwoshadowball .sdata2 regression introduced or reported around PR 2516.
Search for reuse of it_80274740_JObjSetRotationX/Y/Z or it_8027C0F0_JObjSetRotationX/Y/Z patterns in later item matching work.
Look for subsequent PRs converting remaining item files from NonMatching to Matching after remaining item TUs dropped to 21.
Audit symbols.txt local label conversions for grHeal and item sdata2 constants around addresses 0x804DBC98 and 0x804DD790.
